label: The Batch Bakery
subject: Batch baking (oven throughput)
bossHook: A rush of orders, one oven, limited space. Bake them in as few rounds as possible without cramming the oven past its limit — that's throughput.

vocab.hint: Tap a tray to move it between bakes. Fill each bake to the oven's limit — fewer bakes means more cakes per hour.

warmup.title: One Oven, Many Trays
warmup.hook: A slow baker bakes ONE tray at a time. Your oven fits several — slide them into a single bake and they cook together.
warmup.winText: Baked together, one round did the work of three. That's the whole trick of batching.

learn.frame: The oven fits several trays at once — so why would a baker ever bake them one at a time?

reveal.body: Heating the oven is a big fixed cost, paid once per round no matter how many trays are inside. Slide them all into a single bake and that heat is spent ONCE for all of them. That's BATCHING.
reveal.inTheWild: One oven, one heat-up, many trays — the cost of firing it up shared across the whole bake.

pack-tight.title: Fill the Oven
pack-tight.hook: Five trays, oven space for 8 slots a bake. One tray per bake and the oven cools between rounds. Fill each bake to the brim instead.
pack-tight.winText: Two full bakes instead of five half-empty ones — the oven never cooled. More cakes per hour.

failText.underutilize: You spread the trays too thin — the oven idled between tiny bakes. Fill each bake fuller and the round count drops.
failText.overcommit: That bake needed more oven space than you have — trays would burn. Move one to another bake.

reveal.body: The oven cooled between thin bakes because each round costs a full heat-up no matter how full it is. Filling every bake to the brim spreads that cost over more trays — fewer, fuller rounds. That's more cakes per hour.
reveal.inTheWild: A busy bakery never runs a half-empty oven — trays go in the moment there's room.

insights.underutilize: The oven costs a full heat-up whether it holds one tray or eight. One tray per bake pays for that heat with almost nothing to show — full bakes share it across many trays.
insights.overcommit: The oven's size is a hard limit, not a suggestion. Cram past it and nothing cooks right, so overpacking gains you nothing — fill to the edge, then start another bake.

respect-memory.title: The Oven Has a Limit
respect-memory.hook: One big tray takes 5 of 6 slots. Cram greedily and things overflow. Fill tight — but never past the oven's limit.
respect-memory.winText: Every bake full yet under the limit. Fast AND nothing burnt — that's the balance.

failText.underutilize: Fewer, fuller bakes would finish sooner — the oven idled. But watch the limit as you fill.
failText.overcommit: A bake went over the oven's space. When a tray is big, give it room — split the bake.

reveal.body: Fill tight, but the oven's space is a hard limit — a bake can't hold more than fits. The trick is to load each round right up to the edge and no further.
reveal.inTheWild: A pro loads the oven to its last slot without crowding — full heat, nothing burnt.

insights.underutilize: Empty oven slots are cakes you didn't bake this round — the oven runs the same either way, so a half-empty bake is half the output.
insights.overcommit: Going over the oven's space doesn't slow the bake, it ruins it — everything comes out wrong. The limit is the real ceiling: fill up to it, never past.

boss-serve.title: BOSS · Clear the Rush
boss-serve.hook: Six trays, oven space for 8, three bakes to clear the rush. Fill them tight and keep every bake under the limit.
boss-serve.winText: Six trays cleared in three full bakes, nothing burnt. The rush is handled — that's throughput.

failText.underutilize: Too many bakes — the oven idled and orders piled up. Fill the bakes fuller.
failText.overcommit: A bake went over the oven's space. Rebalance the trays so each bake fits.

learn.frame: Fewer bakes means more cakes, but an over-stuffed oven spoils the batch — so where's the perfect load?

reveal.body: The most cakes per hour come from the fullest load the oven allows — one slot under the limit, never over. Too empty wastes the heat; too full ruins the bake. The sweet spot is right at the edge.
reveal.inTheWild: A working kitchen fills every oven to the brim, round after round — that steady flow is throughput.

insights.underutilize: A full heat-up costs the same every round, so fewer, fuller bakes always win — an idle oven is time you can't get back.
insights.overcommit: The oven's limit is absolute: one tray too many and the whole bake is spoiled. The fastest, safest load sits right at the edge, never over it.

batch-drill.title: SIDE-QUEST · Fill One Bake
batch-drill.hook: Drill the habit: three small trays, one bake, room for all of them. Put them ALL in bake 1.
batch-drill.winText: All three in a single bake — the fastest move. Fill first, always.

reveal.body: When it all fits, one bake is always right — one heat-up, paid once, every tray done together. Fill first.
reveal.inTheWild: The habit behind any high-output kitchen: gather the trays before you fire the oven.
